graphView.hierarchySelectedDescription: Showing connections relative to selected node: incoming {{incomingCount}}, outgoing {{outgoingCount}}.
graphView.hierarchyDefaultDescription: Select a file, class, or function to see clear incoming and outgoing dependencies within the hierarchy.
graphView.dependenciesSelectedDescription: Focused dependency subgraph around selected node: incoming {{incomingCount}}, outgoing {{outgoingCount}}.
graphView.dependenciesDefaultDescription: Overview at file and ADR level. Select a node on the graph or in the tree to focus on its neighbors.

mcpSettings.title: MCP Server Settings

analyze_project.title: Analyze Project
analyze_project.description: Indexes the project and loads the graph into CodeMaps. Usually, the agent should call this automatically when changing projects.
analyze_project.recommendedWhen: When the project is not open yet or you need to switch the active workspace.

get_graph_context.title: Get Graph Context
get_graph_context.description: Returns a graph summary or the full graph payload. Useful as a low-level fallback when the raw graph is needed.
get_graph_context.recommendedWhen: When the composite context is insufficient and the agent needs a full snapshot of the graph.

get_node_dependencies.title: Get Node Dependencies
get_node_dependencies.description: Shows incoming and outgoing links of a specific node. Usually needed as a fallback after prepare_change_context.
get_node_dependencies.recommendedWhen: When you need to manually expand the local dependency context.

search_graph.title: Search Graph
search_graph.description: Searches nodes by label or id, optionally filtered by type. Low-level search for ambiguous targets.
search_graph.recommendedWhen: When target resolution in a composite context requires manual refinement.

get_blast_radius.title: Get Blast Radius
get_blast_radius.description: Calculates the direct and transitive impact of changing a node.
get_blast_radius.recommendedWhen: When planning a risky change and needing to assess the impact.

get_architecture_overview.title: Get Architecture Overview
get_architecture_overview.description: Returns layer classifications, cross-layer dependencies, and boundary violations.
get_architecture_overview.recommendedWhen: When analyzing the overall structure or verifying architectural compliance.

get_health_score.title: Get Health Score
get_health_score.description: Computes structural graph health metrics and an overall score.
get_health_score.recommendedWhen: When assessing technical debt or evaluating the impact of a refactor.

detect_patterns.title: Detect Patterns
detect_patterns.description: Finds architectural hotspots and anti-pattern candidates in the graph.
detect_patterns.recommendedWhen: When looking for refactoring opportunities or reviewing code quality.

run_security_scan.title: Run Security Scan
run_security_scan.description: Scans indexed source files for high-risk patterns and suspicious artifacts.
run_security_scan.recommendedWhen: When performing a security review or checking for hardcoded secrets.

search_signatures.title: Search Signatures
search_signatures.description: Searches declaration-like code signatures across indexed source files.
search_signatures.recommendedWhen: When looking for specific function or class definitions.

analyze_pr_impact.title: Analyze PR Impact
analyze_pr_impact.description: Analyzes the architectural blast radius of a Pull Request (or branch comparison).
analyze_pr_impact.recommendedWhen: When reviewing a PR or assessing the impact of a branch before merging.

analyze_activity_heatmap.title: Analyze Activity Heatmap
analyze_activity_heatmap.description: Generates a git churn heatmap to identify frequently modified files (hotspots).
analyze_activity_heatmap.recommendedWhen: When looking for technical debt or unstable code areas.

calculate_blast_radius_v2.title: Calculate Blast Radius V2
calculate_blast_radius_v2.description: Return advanced direct and transitive impact using KuzuDB
calculate_blast_radius_v2.recommendedWhen: When a deep transitive dependency analysis is needed.

prepare_project_context.title: Prepare Project Context
prepare_project_context.description: Creates a project-level mental model so an agent can understand architecture before editing or review.
prepare_project_context.recommendedWhen: Immediately after opening a project to build an architectural understanding.

prepare_task_context.title: Prepare Task Context
prepare_task_context.description: Routes a natural-language user request into the right CodeMaps composite workflow with prepared context.
prepare_task_context.recommendedWhen: When the user describes a problem, feature, or review in natural language.

prepare_change_campaign.title: Prepare Change Campaign
prepare_change_campaign.description: Prepares a phased multi-target migration/refactor context for broad codebase changes.
prepare_change_campaign.recommendedWhen: For mass migrations, library switches, and broad refactoring campaigns.

prepare_change_context.title: Prepare Change Context
prepare_change_context.description: Prepares a high-level change context so an agent can edit code with architectural awareness.
prepare_change_context.recommendedWhen: Before starting a bugfix, feature implementation, or local refactor.

prepare_review_context.title: Prepare Review Context
prepare_review_context.description: Prepares a comprehensive context for code review, including architecture, patterns, and security.
prepare_review_context.recommendedWhen: When reviewing code, assessing architecture, or validating post-change impact.

project_summary.title: Project Summary
project_summary.description: Brief summary of the project graph: root, node count, link count, and node types.

graph_full.title: Full Graph
graph_full.description: Full JSON graph of the project for advanced analysis, client integrations, and debugging.

agent_playbook.title: Agent Playbook
agent_playbook.description: How the agent should use CodeMaps automatically: preferred tools, fallback path, and execution order.

agent_project_brain.title: Project Brain
agent_project_brain.description: Ready-to-use architectural mental model of the current project for an agent-first start without manual tool orchestration.

mcpSettings.streamableHttpServerDescription: Streamable HTTP MCP server for agents and a separate WebSocket channel for UI.
mcpSettings.agentBehaviorDescription: Normal users don't need to remember MCP commands. For a proper agent, `CodeMaps` should now work like this: the agent opens the project itself, requests `prepare_change_context` before edits and `prepare_review_context` for reviews, using low-level tools only as fallback.
mcpSettings.skillSetupStep1: 1. Create a new skill for the agent, e.g. in `.trae/skills/codemaps-agent-autopilot/SKILL.md`.
mcpSettings.skillSetupStep2: 2. Copy the template below without changes.
mcpSettings.skillSetupStep3: 3. After this, any agent will be able to work continuously through `prepare_task_context`, `prepare_change_campaign`, `prepare_change_context`, and `prepare_review_context`, rather than manually calling low-level tools.
mcpSettings.traeDescription: HTTP MCP server is added by URL. For local CodeMaps use the streamable HTTP endpoint.
mcpSettings.cursorDescription: For Cursor set transport to streamable HTTP and use the same local endpoint.
mcpSettings.claudeCodeDescription: Claude Code is easiest to connect via CLI command to immediately register the server in the client.
mcpSettings.skillTemplateDescription: If an agent is connected to `codemaps` but lacks a permanent instruction to work through it as an architectural brain, it may use MCP too weakly or too late. This `SKILL.md` template fixes proper behavior: `CodeMaps` first, then local reading and edits.

filterPanel.hierarchyDescription: Project structure and nesting. Connections are better read after selecting a specific node.
filterPanel.dependenciesDescription: Dependency overview at file and ADR level. After selecting a node, its local subgraph is shown.

persistence.clearCacheConfirm: Are you sure you want to clear the local graph cache? This will require re-analyzing the project.
persistence.enablePersistenceDescription: Save graph to local database for instant loading on next open

error.diskFull: Disk space is running low. Graph may not be saved.
error.corrupted: Graph database appears corrupted. Re-analyzing project...

activityHeatmap.title: Activity Heatmap
activityHeatmap.noData: No activity data found. Make sure this is a git repository.
